name: Asian Marinated Pork Chops
instructions:
1. In a large, heavy-duty resealable plastic bag, combine the soy sauce, brown sugar, minced garlic, ginger powder, ground cumin, and red chili paste. Seal the bag and massage it with your fingers for about a minute to mix all the ingredients thoroughly and dissolve the brown sugar completely.

2. Add the pork chops to the marinade, making sure they are fully submerged and coated. Seal the bag, pressing out as much air as possible, and refrigerate. Allow the pork chops to marinate for 30 to 45 minutes, turning the bag occasionally to ensure even coverage.

3. While the pork marinates, preheat an outdoor grill to medium heat. Once hot, lightly oil the grate using a paper towel dipped in cooking oil to prevent sticking.

4. Remove the pork chops from the marinade and discard the remaining marinade. Shake off any excess liquid and place the chops on the preheated grill. Grill until the pork is beautifully browned with distinct grill marks and no longer pink in the center, about 5 to 7 minutes per side. For best results, use an instant-read meat thermometer inserted into the thickest part of a chop—it should read at least 145°F (63°C). Let the chops rest for a few minutes before serving.
simpleInstructions:
1. Combine marinade ingredients in a bag and massage to dissolve sugar.

2. Add pork chops and marinate for 30-45 minutes in the fridge.

3. Preheat and oil your grill to medium heat.

4. Grill chops until browned with nice marks, 5-7 minutes per side, then rest and serve!
